source: Sunlo scraped ideas uploaded by user

ideas.name: Snapchat
ideas.description: Frame the email like a Snapchat story: a sequence of full-bleed 'snaps' that reveal the offer, capped with a streak counter that rewards repeat opens or purchases.
ideas.whyItWorks: The reveal mechanic drives scroll depth, and the 'this disappears' framing manufactures urgency without a literal countdown timer.
ideas.ecomTieIn: Use a streak counter to reward repeat buyers — every order keeps the streak (and the perk) alive.
ideas.heroImagePrompt:
Create a portrait hero image for a marketing email — 600px wide, 3:4 ratio (about 600 x 800px).

STYLE: Snapchat. Frame the email like a Snapchat story: a sequence of full-bleed 'snaps' that reveal the offer, capped with a streak counter that rewards repeat opens or purchases.

SUBJECT: Make [ATTACH PRODUCT PHOTO] from [BRAND NAME] the unmistakable hero, front and centre, above the fold. Stay on-brand for [BRAND NAME] — use its logo and brand colours ([BRAND COLOURS]).

COMPOSITION: Disappearing 'story' reveal with a loyalty streak counter. Leave clean negative space at the top for a headline and at the bottom for a CTA button.

ON-IMAGE TEXT (optional): headline "[YOUR HEADLINE]"; CTA button "Tap to view".

TECHNICAL: portrait 3:4, sharp focus on the product, mobile-inbox friendly, no watermark, no stock-photo feel, do not invent logos or brand names. Attach the brand's product photo / assets before generating.

ideas.name: Spotify Playlist
ideas.description: Turn the email into a Spotify playlist or 'For You' mix: products become tracks, bundles become playlists, and recommendations feel personally curated.
ideas.whyItWorks: The familiar music UI gives every item a role in the reader's 'setlist', which is perfect for bundles, drops, or mood-based shopping.
ideas.ecomTieIn: Name product bundles like playlists — e.g. 'The Airport Outfit Mix' or 'The Sunday Reset Set'.

ideas.name: YouTube Thumbnail
ideas.description: Build the email like a click-worthy YouTube thumbnail and video page: bold promise, big product visual, playful annotations, and a 'watch next' product row.
ideas.whyItWorks: Thumbnail language is designed to stop scrolling fast; it helps the hero make one instantly readable promise.
ideas.ecomTieIn: Use 'watch next' rows to recommend complementary products or next-best sellers.

ideas.name: Instagram Carousel
ideas.description: Create an email that behaves like a swipeable Instagram carousel: each email section is a 'slide' with a tight hook, one visual, and one takeaway.
ideas.whyItWorks: Carousel rhythm trains the reader to keep scrolling, while the single-idea-per-section structure keeps the email mobile-friendly.
ideas.ecomTieIn: Use each slide as a different reason to buy, objection solved, or colourway reveal.

ideas.name: TikTok Trend
ideas.description: Structure the email around a trend-style reveal: 'POV', 'things that just make sense', or 'things I didn't know I needed until...' adapted for ecommerce.
ideas.whyItWorks: Trend framing creates instant familiarity and gives the product a cultural reason to exist beyond 'new in'.
ideas.ecomTieIn: Use trend language to make product benefits feel discovered rather than explained.
